Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: I made a zero-copy coroutine tracer to find my scheduler's lost wakeups 22 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Graphics Programming Resources 121 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Textadept 156 upvotes, 28 comments
  4. Elevator Saga: The elevator programming game (2015) 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Speculative Speculative Decoding (SSD) 51 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. Launch HN: Cekura (YC F24) – Testing and monitoring for voice and chat AI agents 86 upvotes, 20 comments
  7. Voxile: A ray-traced game made in its own engine and programming language 218 upvotes, 62 comments
  8. Show HN: Stacked Game of Life 55 upvotes, 12 comments
  9. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 871 upvotes, 311 comments
  10. On the Design of Programming Languages (1974) [pdf] 55 upvotes, 13 comments
  11. RE#: how we built the fastest regex engine in F# 57 upvotes, 15 comments
  12. Claude's Cycles [pdf] 663 upvotes, 285 comments
  13. Modern Illustration: Archive of illustration from c.1950-1975 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Iran war wreaking havoc on shipping and air cargo, could create global delays 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Agentic Engineering Patterns 229 upvotes, 108 comments
  16. Better JIT for Postgres 85 upvotes, 34 comments
  17. A CPU that runs entirely on GPU 134 upvotes, 61 comments
  18. You can use newline characters in URLs 93 upvotes, 40 comments
  19. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 129 upvotes, 64 comments
  20. Reverse-Engineering the Wetware: Spiking Networks and the End of Matrix Math 29 upvotes, 11 comments
  21. Indefinite Book Club Hiatus 57 upvotes, 28 comments
  22. Weave – A language aware merge algorithm based on entities 143 upvotes, 88 comments
  23. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 13 upvotes, 4 comments
  24. Bet on German Train Delays 116 upvotes, 85 comments
  25. TikTok will not introduce end-to-end encryption, saying it makes users less safe 282 upvotes, 271 comments
  26. Users fume over Outlook.com email 'carnage' 44 upvotes, 33 comments
  27. MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max 812 upvotes, 874 comments
  28. When AI writes the software, who verifies it? 256 upvotes, 262 comments
  29. My spicy take on vibe coding for PMs 128 upvotes, 126 comments
  30. Jiga (YC W21) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 4 March 2026. Source here.
